title: flydubai vs Air Arabia from Pakistan — Which Budget Airline is Cheaper in 2026?
description: flydubai vs Air Arabia from Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad to Dubai — baggage, prices in PKR, punctuality and which to pick on each date.
keywords: air arabia karachi to dubai cheap
intro:
Both flydubai and Air Arabia fly direct from Pakistan to Dubai and are consistently the two cheapest options on the route. But which one is actually cheaper — and when? The answer changes by date, city, and season. This guide compares flydubai and Air Arabia head-to-head on the routes that matter most: Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad to Dubai.

FlightRate scrapes prices from both airlines three times daily, so we see the real PKR difference between them every single day. The short answer: flydubai wins from Lahore and Islamabad, Air Arabia wins from Karachi — but not always.

sections.h2: flydubai vs Air Arabia — Quick Comparison
sections.content:
Before diving into prices, here's what separates the two carriers. flydubai is a Dubai government-owned budget airline flying from Dubai International (Terminal 2). Air Arabia is a Sharjah-based budget carrier flying from Sharjah Airport (SHJ) — 30 minutes from Dubai by road. Both allow 20 kg check-in economy, no meals included, similar aircraft (Boeing 737 vs Airbus A320).

The destination airport matters: Dubai International (flydubai) is more convenient if you're staying in Dubai. Sharjah (Air Arabia) requires a taxi or bus to Dubai — budget PKR 2,500–4,000 for transport. If staying in Sharjah or Ajman, Air Arabia's airport is actually more convenient.

On-time performance: flydubai and Air Arabia are broadly similar — both significantly better than PIA on these routes. Flight duration is almost identical: Karachi–Dubai about 3h, Lahore–Dubai about 3.5h, Islamabad–Dubai about 3.5h.

sections.h2: Price Comparison: flydubai vs Air Arabia from Karachi
sections.content:
From Karachi (KHI), Air Arabia has historically been the slightly cheaper option. Air Arabia operates KHI–SHJ (Sharjah) rather than KHI–DXB (Dubai), giving it a structural cost advantage. Typical economy fares: PKR 45,000–75,000 on Air Arabia vs PKR 50,000–85,000 on flydubai, though this varies significantly by date.

However, flydubai also flies KHI–DXB direct, and on certain dates (especially midweek, early morning departures) flydubai undercuts Air Arabia. The only way to know which is cheaper on your specific date is to check both — FlightRate does this automatically and shows you the lower price.

Add-ons to compare: both charge for meals, seat selection, and extra baggage. Air Arabia charges PKR 3,000–5,000 per 5 kg extra baggage pre-booked. flydubai is similar at PKR 3,500–5,500 per 5 kg. Neither includes airport transfers in the ticket price.

sections.h2: flydubai vs Air Arabia from Lahore
sections.content:
From Lahore (LHE), flydubai is more frequently the cheaper option. Air Arabia does not fly Lahore–Sharjah directly (it operates via limited routes), while flydubai has multiple daily Lahore–Dubai flights with strong competition on pricing.

Typical flydubai LHE–DXB fares: PKR 55,000–90,000. Compared against full-service alternatives (Emirates PKR 80,000–120,000, PIA PKR 65,000–100,000), flydubai consistently undercuts. If Air Arabia is not flying direct from Lahore on your date, flydubai is effectively the default budget choice.

From Lahore, early morning flydubai departures (typically 2–4 AM) are cheapest. Night flights allow arriving in Dubai early morning, maximising your day — popular with workers and business travellers.

sections.h2: Which Is Better for Families with Extra Luggage?
sections.content:
Both flydubai and Air Arabia base economy tickets include only 20 kg check-in. For families carrying 25–30 kg per person, the budget airlines stop being cheap very quickly once excess baggage fees are added.

A family of 4 travelling with 25 kg each: 20 kg included, 5 kg excess per person. On flydubai or Air Arabia, that's PKR 12,000–20,000 in extra baggage — potentially making Emirates (30 kg included) the better deal overall. Always compare total trip cost: base fare + baggage + transfer to Dubai (for Air Arabia via Sharjah).

For solo travellers or couples with light luggage under 18–20 kg: both airlines are genuinely the cheapest option and the savings over Emirates can be PKR 15,000–30,000 per person.

sections.h2: Reliability and Schedule — Which Budget Airline Is More Consistent?
sections.content:
Both flydubai and Air Arabia have good punctuality records on Pakistan routes compared to the industry average. flydubai's parent is Emirates — it uses the same maintenance and operational standards. Air Arabia is the Middle East's first and largest low-cost carrier, with a long operational history in the region.

flydubai offers more flight times from Karachi and Lahore — typically 3–5 departures daily. Air Arabia has fewer frequencies but often includes very early morning options. For flexibility (ability to change flights), both charge change fees of approximately PKR 5,000–10,000. Buy flexible fares if your travel dates might change — the fee difference between basic and flexible is usually PKR 3,000–6,000.

sections.h2: How to Always Find the Cheapest Option Between flydubai and Air Arabia
sections.content:
Don't assume either airline is always cheaper — prices change daily and the difference can swing PKR 8,000–20,000 depending on date. The only reliable method is to check both on your specific travel date.

FlightRate compares all airlines including flydubai and Air Arabia in real-time PKR prices. Check at least 3 weeks before travel — both airlines release promotional fares at this window. Tuesday and Wednesday departures are consistently 10–20% cheaper than Friday–Sunday on both airlines. Early morning flights (before 6 AM) are typically the cheapest on any given day.

Once you find the best price, book directly on the airline's website or contact FlightRate via WhatsApp — we confirm current fares and complete the booking in PKR within 7 minutes.

faq.q: Is flydubai or Air Arabia cheaper from Karachi to Dubai?
faq.a: Air Arabia is slightly cheaper from Karachi on average because it flies to Sharjah (lower airport costs) rather than Dubai International. However, flydubai undercuts Air Arabia on specific dates — particularly midweek and early morning flights. FlightRate compares both daily so you always see the real cheaper option.

faq.q: Does Air Arabia fly from Lahore to Dubai?
faq.a: Air Arabia has limited direct service from Lahore. flydubai is the main budget carrier on the Lahore–Dubai route with multiple daily flights. If you're departing from Lahore, compare flydubai against PIA and Emirates rather than Air Arabia.

faq.q: What is flydubai baggage allowance from Pakistan?
faq.a: flydubai economy allows 20 kg check-in baggage from all Pakistani cities. Cabin baggage is 7 kg. Extra baggage can be pre-purchased online for approximately PKR 3,500–5,500 per 5 kg — always buy online, not at the airport where rates are 2–3× higher.

faq.q: Is Air Arabia Sharjah airport far from Dubai?
faq.a: Sharjah airport is approximately 30–40 minutes by road from central Dubai (varies by traffic). Taxi costs PKR 2,500–4,000. If you're staying in Sharjah or Ajman, Air Arabia's airport is actually more convenient than Dubai International. Factor in transfer cost when comparing Air Arabia vs flydubai total prices.

faq.q: Which budget airline has better service — flydubai or Air Arabia?
faq.a: Both are no-frills budget carriers with similar service levels. flydubai has a slight edge in inflight entertainment (screens on newer aircraft). Air Arabia has a longer track record as the region's first low-cost carrier. For a 3–3.5 hour flight to Dubai, the difference in service is minimal — price and schedule matter more.
